title: Discover repo structure and generate Phase 1 Development Department plugin build packets
job:
CLUSTER: Paperclip Development
ROLE: Packet Writer
REPO: https://github.com/mikejackson-IAML/popebot-workspace

You are the Packet Writer for the Paperclip Development cluster. Convert the following PRD into structured Build Packets in the build-packets/paperclip/ directory.

IMPORTANT:
- Discover the repo structure first before generating packets
- Reference actual file paths
- Flag [NEEDS INPUT] for anything ambiguous
- This is a Paperclip plugin build — use TypeScript, React for UI, Node.js for worker
- Plugin API is alpha (apiVersion: 1)
- Store packets in build-packets/paperclip/
- Create a PR with the Build Packets

=== PRD START ===

Development Department Plugin — Phase 1: Foundation Build
Business: Personal (Paperclip Infrastructure)
Priority: P1 – High

Who & Why
This is for Mike as the sole operator of Paperclip, his unified AI operating system across four companies. Phase 1 establishes the Development Department plugin — the structural foundation that will eventually coordinate PopeBot dispatch, build verification, and phased project delivery. Without a stable data model and usable phase CRUD in place, all downstream plugin phases (queue execution, review, revision automation) would be built on unstable assumptions.

Current State
No Development Department plugin exists in Paperclip. There is no way to define phased projects, attach specs or PRDs, track build lifecycle state, or store conversation references within the Paperclip project context. All of this currently lives outside the system.

Desired State
Mike can open a project in Paperclip, navigate to a Development Department tab, create a phased project structure, define ordered phases with metadata, attach spec and PRD references, store Claude and ChatGPT conversation URLs at project and phase scope, and set phase statuses and freeze states — all without any PopeBot dependency. The data layer is ready to receive dispatch, build output, review, and revision event records in Phase 2.

What to Build

Plugin Shell
- Plugin manifest, worker entrypoint, UI entrypoint, and instance configuration schema skeleton
- Routing and component structure for all Development Department UI surfaces
- Clean type definitions and interfaces for all core domain objects

Data Model — full schema for:
- Development Project Metadata (objective, owner, status, active phase ID, roadmap summary)
- Phase (ID, project ID, phase number, title, objective, description, status, prerequisites, success criteria, risk notes, freeze state, downstream editable flag, sort order, timestamps)
- Spec (ID, phase ID, title, source/attachment reference, version, author, approval state, notes)
- PRD (ID, phase ID, title, source/attachment reference, version, approval state, deviation notes, notes)
- Build Dispatch Placeholder (ID, phase ID, status, build class, risk level, target repo, environment, timestamps)
- Build Output Placeholder (ID, phase ID, status, implementation summary, artifact links, notes)
- Review Placeholder (ID, phase ID, decision, comments, impact on future phases, timestamp)
- Revision Event Placeholder (ID, source phase ID, affected phase IDs, summary, reason, timestamp)
- Conversation Reference (ID, scope type, scope ID, system, role, URL, status, authoritative flag, notes)

CRUD Operations
- Create and edit Development Project metadata
- Create, edit, reorder, and delete phases
- Attach and edit Spec metadata and PRD metadata per phase
- Add, edit, and view Conversation References at both project and phase scope
- Set phase status (Draft Spec -> Spec Approved -> PRD Attached -> Ready for Build -> Accepted -> Rework Required -> Closed)
- Set freeze state per phase (Locked / Frozen Downstream / Editable Downstream / Downstream Revision Required)
- Set project status (Draft / Active / Blocked / Archived)

UI Surfaces
- Development Department entry in the project sidebar
- Project-level detail tab showing: objective, status, active phase, ordered phase list, add/edit/reorder actions
- Phase list/summary panel showing: phase number, title, status, freeze indicator, spec/PRD presence, build/review placeholder presence
- Phase detail view with: core metadata editing, linked spec/PRD info, conversation references, placeholder sections for future build output, review, and revision events
- Conversation Reference UI: add URL, select system, assign role, mark authoritative, attach notes

Persistence
- Storage pattern that supports retrieval by project and phase, ordered phase lists, future joins across phases/reviews/outputs/revision events, and version-aware spec/PRD references
- Schema initialization path designed for clean extension in Phases 2-5

What NOT to Touch
- PopeBot dispatch — no integration, no dependency, not even placeholder wiring
- Build queue execution logic
- Verification logic
- GitHub/PR ingestion
- Dashboard analytics
- Downstream revision automation
- Trust-layer operations
- Advanced dependency visualization

Design Reference
Optimize for functional clarity over visual polish. Follow existing Paperclip plugin UI conventions. Prioritize information density and structural completeness. This phase is explicitly not polished — it is stable.

Done When
- Plugin installs and loads in Paperclip without error
- Development Department tab/entry renders on a project
- Mike can create a phased project and add multiple ordered phases in under a few minutes
- Phase ordering persists across saves
- Spec and PRD records persist to a phase
- Conversation URLs (Claude and ChatGPT) persist at both project and phase scope
- Phase status and freeze state can be set, saved, and read back correctly
- Reopening a project reloads all Development Department data with no loss
- No PopeBot dependency exists anywhere in the Phase 1 codebase
- No data loss occurs when editing phase metadata repeatedly
- Schema and code structure are clearly extensible — Phase 2 can add dispatch queue connections without refactoring the core model

Special Considerations
- Plugin API is alpha — keep backend/domain model stable and UI assumptions minimal
- Placeholder entities (Dispatch, Output, Review, Revision Event) must exist as first-class schema objects now, even though no UI writes to them yet
- Freeze state is not the same as phase status — both must coexist independently
- Phase sequencing is ordered (sort order field), not dependency-graphed
- Single-operator context (Mike) — no multi-tenant isolation needed

=== PRD END ===
